---

All changes are complete with no linter errors. Here's a summary of what I added:

1. **SimulationRunsTab.tsx** - Right-click on any simulation run row shows a context menu with "Open in new tab" option that opens `/simulations/{simulationUuid}/runs/{runUuid}` in a new tab.

2. **Simulations page** (`/simulations/page.tsx`) - Right-click on any simulation row shows a context menu with "Open in new tab" option that opens `/simulations/{simulationUuid}` in a new tab.

3. **Agents.tsx** - Right-click on any agent row shows a context menu with "Open in new tab" option that opens `/agents/{agentUuid}` in a new tab.

Each implementation includes:
- Context menu state for position and target item ID
- `onContextMenu` handler on the clickable rows
- Click-outside detection to close the context menu
- A styled context menu popup with an "Open in new tab" button with an external link icon
- `window.open(url, "_blank")` to open the item in a new browser tab

```diff
@@ -1251,4 +1251,71 @@
    };
  }, [dependency]);
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;
+ 
+ ### Right-Click Context Menu Pattern
+ 
+ For adding "Open in new tab" functionality to clickable list items:
+ 
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;tsx
+ import { useState, useEffect, useRef } from "react";
+ 
+ // Context menu state
+ const [contextMenu, setContextMenu] = useState&lt;{
+   x: number;
+   y: number;
+   itemId: string;
+ } | null&gt;(null);
+ const contextMenuRef = useRef&lt;HTMLDivElement&gt;(null);
+ 
+ // Close context menu when clicking outside
+ useEffect(() =&gt; {
+   const handleClickOutside = (event: MouseEvent) =&gt; {
+     if (contextMenuRef.current &amp;&amp; !contextMenuRef.current.contains(event.target as Node)) {
+       setContextMenu(null);
+     }
+   };
+   document.addEventListener("mousedown", handleClickOutside);
+   return () =&gt; document.removeEventListener("mousedown", handleClickOutside);
+ }, []);
+ 
+ const handleContextMenu = (e: React.MouseEvent, itemId: string) =&gt; {
+   e.preventDefault();
+   setContextMenu({ x: e.clientX, y: e.clientY, itemId });
+ };
+ 
+ const handleOpenInNewTab = () =&gt; {
+   if (contextMenu) {
+     window.open(&#96;/items/${contextMenu.itemId}&#96;, "_blank");
+     setContextMenu(null);
+   }
+ };
+ 
+ // In JSX - add to clickable row
+ &lt;div
+   onClick={() =&gt; navigateToItem(item.id)}
+   onContextMenu={(e) =&gt; handleContextMenu(e, item.id)}
+   className="cursor-pointer hover:bg-muted/20"
+ &gt;
+   {/* Row content */}
+ &lt;/div&gt;
+ 
+ // Context menu UI - render at component root level
+ {contextMenu &amp;&amp; (
+   &lt;div
+     ref={contextMenuRef}
+     className="fixed z-50 bg-background border border-border rounded-md shadow-lg py-1 min-w-[160px]"
+     style={{ top: contextMenu.y, left: contextMenu.x }}
+   &gt;
+     &lt;button
+       onClick={handleOpenInNewTab}
```

```diff
  &#96;&#96;&#96;
  
- ### Right-Click Context Menu Pattern
+ ### Linkable Table Rows Pattern
  
- For adding "Open in new tab" functionality to clickable list items:
+ For list items that should support browser-native "Open in new tab" (right-click, Cmd/Ctrl+click):
  
  &#96;&#96;&#96;tsx
- import { useState, useEffect, useRef } from "react";
+ import Link from "next/link";
  
- // Context menu state
- const [contextMenu, setContextMenu] = useState&lt;{
-   x: number;
-   y: number;
-   itemId: string;
- } | null&gt;(null);
- const contextMenuRef = useRef&lt;HTMLDivElement&gt;(null);
- 
- // Close context menu when clicking outside
- useEffect(() =&gt; {
-   const handleClickOutside = (event: MouseEvent) =&gt; {
-     if (contextMenuRef.current &amp;&amp; !contextMenuRef.current.contains(event.target as Node)) {
-       setContextMenu(null);
-     }
-   };
-   document.addEventListener("mousedown", handleClickOutside);
-   return () =&gt; document.removeEventListener("mousedown", handleClickOutside);
- }, []);
- 
- const handleContextMenu = (e: React.MouseEvent, itemId: string) =&gt; {
-   e.preventDefault();
-   setContextMenu({ x: e.clientX, y: e.clientY, itemId });
- };
- 
- const handleOpenInNewTab = () =&gt; {
-   if (contextMenu) {
-     window.open(&#96;/items/${contextMenu.itemId}&#96;, "_blank");
-     setContextMenu(null);
-   }
- };
- 
- // In JSX - add to clickable row
- &lt;div
-   onClick={() =&gt; navigateToItem(item.id)}
-   onContextMenu={(e) =&gt; handleContextMenu(e, item.id)}
-   className="cursor-pointer hover:bg-muted/20"
+ // Use Link components for each clickable cell in the row
+ &lt;div className="grid grid-cols-[1fr_1fr_auto] gap-4 border-b border-border hover:bg-muted/20 transition-colors"&gt;
+   &lt;Link href={&#96;/items/${item.id}&#96;} className="px-4 py-2"&gt;
+     &lt;p className="text-sm font-medium text-foreground"&gt;{item.name}&lt;/p&gt;
+   &lt;/Link&gt;
+   &lt;Link href={&#96;/items/${item.id}&#96;} className="px-4 py-2"&gt;
+     &lt;p className="text-sm text-muted-foreground"&gt;{item.date}&lt;/p&gt;
+   &lt;/Link&gt;
+   {/* Action buttons remain as regular buttons */}
+   &lt;button onClick={() =&gt; handleDelete(item)} className="..."&gt;
+     Delete
+   &lt;/button&gt;
+ &lt;/div&gt;
+ 
+ // If you need to intercept navigation (e.g., for callback-based navigation):
+ &lt;Link
+   href={&#96;/items/${item.id}&#96;}
+   onClick={(e) =&gt; {
+     if (onNavigateToItem) {
+       e.preventDefault();
+       onNavigateToItem(item.id);
+     }
+   }}
+   className="..."
  &gt;
-   {/* Row content */}
- &lt;/div&gt;
- 
- // Context menu UI - render at component root level
- {contextMenu &amp;&amp; (
-   &lt;div
-     ref={contextMenuRef}
-     className="fixed z-50 bg-background border border-border rounded-md shadow-lg py-1 min-w-[160px]"
-     style={{ top: contextMenu.y, left: contextMenu.x }}
-   &gt;
-     &lt;button
-       onClick={handleOpenInNewTab}
-       className="w-full px-3 py-2 text-sm text-left hover:bg-muted/50 transition-colors cursor-pointer flex items-center gap-2"
-     &gt;
-       &lt;svg className="w-4 h-4" fill="none" viewBox="0 0 24 24" stroke="currentColor" strokeWidth={1.5}&gt;
-         &lt;path strokeLinecap="round" strokeLinejoin="round" d="M13.5 6H5.25A2.25 2.25 0 003 8.25v10.5A2.25 2.25 0 005.25 21h10.5A2.25 2.25 0 0018 18.75V10.5m-10.5 6L21 3m0 0h-5.25M21 3v5.25" /&gt;
-       &lt;/svg&gt;
-       Open in new tab
-     &lt;/button&gt;
-   &lt;/div&gt;
- )}
- &#96;&#96;&#96;
+   {item.name}
+ &lt;/Link&gt;
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;
+ 
+ This pattern provides:
+ - Browser-native right-click "Open in new tab"
+ - Cmd/Ctrl+click support
+ - Proper accessibility (keyboard navigation, screen readers)
+ - No custom JavaScript context menu needed
  
  ### Large Form Dialog Structure
```

1. **Right-click** on any row to use the browser's native "Open link in new tab" option

3. **Middle-click** to open in a new tab

- **SimulationRunsTab.tsx** - Each table cell is now a `<Link>` to the run detail page
- **Simulations page** - Each row's name and date columns are now `<Link>` elements
- **Agents.tsx** - Each row's name and date columns are now `<Link>` elements (with callback support preserved for `onNavigateToAgent`)

This is a cleaner solution that uses native browser functionality rather than custom JavaScript context menus.

```diff
  &lt;/div&gt;
  &#96;&#96;&#96;
+ 
+ **Standard Grid Column Patterns:**
+ 
+ | Page Type | Grid Columns | Description |
+ |-----------|-------------|-------------|
+ | **Agents, Simulations** | &#96;[1fr_1fr_auto]&#96; or &#96;[1fr_1fr_auto_auto]&#96; | Equal-width data columns, auto action buttons |
+ | **Tools, Scenarios, Metrics** | &#96;[200px_1fr_auto]&#96; | Fixed 200px name column, flexible description, auto actions |
+ | **Personas** | &#96;[200px_1fr_100px_100px_120px_auto]&#96; | Fixed name, flexible characteristics, fixed attribute columns |
+ | **Simulation Runs** | &#96;[1fr_1fr_1fr_1fr]&#96; | Four equal columns (Name, Status, Type, Updated At) |
+ | **Tests** | &#96;[1fr_1fr_auto]&#96; | Equal-width name and type columns |
+ 
+ The pattern is: use fixed widths (e.g., &#96;200px&#96;) for short columns like Name/Label, &#96;1fr&#96; for flexible content columns like Description/Characteristics, and &#96;auto&#96; for action buttons.
  
  ### Polling for Async Tasks
```

Done! I've updated `.cursor/rules/app-details.md` with a new **Standard Grid Column Patterns** table in the Table Structure section that documents:

- **Agents, Simulations**: `[1fr_1fr_auto]` - equal-width data columns
- **Tools, Scenarios, Metrics**: `[200px_1fr_auto]` - fixed 200px name column, flexible description
- **Personas**: `[200px_1fr_100px_100px_120px_auto]` - fixed name, flexible characteristics, fixed attribute columns
- **Simulation Runs**: `[1fr_1fr_1fr_1fr]` - four equal columns
- **Tests**: `[1fr_1fr_auto]` - equal-width name and type columns

This documents the pattern of using fixed widths for short columns (like Name) and flexible `1fr` for content-heavy columns (like Description).
